trade.summary: Bilateral trade has been significant — Canada exports lentils, potash, timber, and coal to India; India exports pharmaceuticals, IT services, textiles, and chemicals to Canada. Trade has been negatively impacted by the diplomatic crisis but continues.
disputes: Diplomatic crisis has complicated trade negotiations

diplomatic.summary: Canada-India relations reached a historic low in 2023 following Canadian PM Trudeau's allegation that Indian government agents were involved in the killing of Sikh separatist leader Hardeep Singh Nijjar on Canadian soil. India denied involvement, expelled Canadian diplomats, and both countries recalled their high commissioners. The crisis reflects deep tensions over Khalistan activism among Canadian Sikhs.
recent_incidents: Alleged Indian state involvement in Nijjar assassination (2023)
India suspended visa services to Canada

military.summary: No military alliance. Military cooperation has been effectively suspended during the diplomatic crisis. Canada and India had been developing defense ties before 2023. Canada has called India's alleged actions state-sponsored terrorism.
incidents: Alleged Indian intelligence operations on Canadian soil

financial.summary: Despite political tensions, economic ties remain significant. Large Indian-Canadian community (1.8 million) maintains commercial links. Canadian pension funds (CPPIB, OMERS) have major investments in India's infrastructure. Indian IT companies have significant Canadian operations.

energy.summary: Canada exports uranium to India under nuclear safeguards agreements. Canadian nuclear expertise (CANDU reactors) is historically significant for India. Energy trade has continued but is politically sensitive.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been maintained despite political tensions, though some joint programs are under review. Large Indian-Canadian academic diaspora bridges both countries. Collaboration in IT, engineering, and pharmaceuticals.
